drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies - refer to rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).
In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (38mg/ml).

In 500ml glucose 5% over 120 minutes. Rate may be increased to 360 minutes if acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia occurs. Dose banded per national dose bands (5mg/ml). Exfoliant - extravasation risk.

dose_modifications.notes: Limited information available; probably little need to adjust dose. Use clinical judgement.
For moderate renal impairment treat at normal dose and monitor. If CrCl <30ml/min then dose reduce according to toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Oxaliplatin neurosensory toxicity NCI-CTC grade 2 persisting >7 days
Oxaliplatin neurosensory toxicity NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity (other)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below then reduce to 75% of original dose. Gemcitabine may be reduced to 750mg/m² to maintain dose intensity.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
special_warnings: Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Rituximab associated with severe cytokine release syndrome (CRS) - typically within 1-2 hours of first infusion. Emergency medications must be available.
Risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) with rituximab - monitor for neurological symptoms. Suspend if PML suspected.
Oxaliplatin: cumulative peripheral neuropathy. Increase infusion duration to 360 minutes if acute laryngopharyngeal dysaesthesia.
Gemcitabine is radiosensitising.
FBC, LFTs, U&Es including magnesium and calcium required prior to day 1.
Anti-infective prophylaxis: co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri, aciclovir 400mg BD (high risk patients).
Allopurinol 300mg OD for 7 days first cycle (TLS prophylaxis).
Growth factor support recommended from day 5 of cycle.

disease_site: Follicular / Mantle Cell / Marginal Zone / Lymphoplasmacytic Lymphoma

drugs.notes: Fixed dose 1400mg SC. Administered over approximately 5 minutes into abdominal wall. Only for patients who have previously received IV rituximab. Needle must be attached immediately prior to administration. Inject into abdominal wall; avoid red, bruised, tender or hard areas and areas with moles or scars.

dose_modifications.notes: Do not administer SC rituximab until counts recover to these minimum thresholds.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for SC rituximab.

pre_medications: Non-sedating oral antihistamine (e.g. cetirizine 10mg, loratadine 10mg, fexofenadine 120mg, or acrivastine 8mg) 15-30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus when required for infusion-related reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for bronchospasm
special_warnings: FIXED DOSE 1400mg SC - NOT interchangeable with IV rituximab formulation. Do NOT substitute IV rituximab for SC rituximab.
Dose reductions are NOT permitted for SC rituximab.
Must only be given to patients who have previously received IV rituximab.
Starting 12 weeks after the last dose of induction chemotherapy.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Consider monitoring serum immunoglobulin levels.
Other SC medicines should preferably be injected at different sites.

drugs.notes: Fixed dose 1400mg SC. Administered over approximately 5 minutes into abdominal wall. Only for patients who have previously received IV rituximab. Needle must be attached immediately prior to administration. Inject into abdominal wall; avoid red, bruised, tender or hard areas and areas with moles or scars.

dose_modifications.notes: Do not administer SC rituximab until counts recover to these minimum thresholds.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for SC rituximab.

Paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus when required for infusion-related reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for bronchospasm
special_warnings: FIXED DOSE 1400mg SC - NOT interchangeable with IV rituximab formulation. Do NOT substitute IV rituximab for SC rituximab.
Dose reductions are NOT permitted for SC rituximab.
Must only be given to patients who have previously received IV rituximab.
Starting 8 weeks after the last dose of induction chemotherapy.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Consider monitoring serum immunoglobulin levels.
Other SC medicines should preferably be injected at different sites.
Indication: previously UNTREATED follicular/mantle cell/marginal zone/lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma (contrast with 12-weekly which is for relapsed/refractory).

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies - refer to rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for rituximab weekly induction.
Use with caution or consider reducing cell load by other means prior to rituximab infusion to reduce risk of severe CRS.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for rituximab weekly induction.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Risk of severe cytokine release syndrome (CRS) especially with WBC >25 x10⁹/L or bulky disease.
Risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Allopurinol 300mg OD oral for first 21 days of treatment (TLS prophylaxis).
4-dose induction course; each dose given on days 1, 8, 15, 22 (7-day cycle x 4).

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).
IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.
IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Vesicant. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
In 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Irritant.

50mg once daily oral days 1-28 inclusive in cycle 1. From cycle 2 onwards: 50mg on alternate days from day 29 onwards (i.e. alternate days). To be taken in the morning with or after food. Self-administered on the morning of treatment - check patient has taken prior to chemotherapy.

10,000 International Units/m² IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 1000 IU (up if halfway). Maximum cumulative dose 500,000 IU in patients <60 years (refer to SPC for older patients). GCSF must be omitted on days of bleomycin administration to avoid pulmonary toxicity.
IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Folinic acid rescue 15mg oral 6-hourly for 6 doses starting 24 hours after methotrexate. Inflammitant.
IV bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Maximum dose 2mg. Rounded to nearest 0.1mg (up if halfway). Vesicant. NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose. Day 8 and 22 given irrespective of haematological counts.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-0.9 x10⁹/L (day 1/15) - 1st occurrence, curative intent, WHO PS ≤2, no prior GCSF
dose_modifications.notes: Administer 100% with prophylactic growth factors. Alternative: delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then give 75% with prophylactic growth factors.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then reduce by further 25% of original dose with prophylactic growth factors.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (day 1/15) - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then give 75% with prophylactic growth factors.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (day 1/15) - 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then give 50% with prophylactic growth factors.
Delay until platelets ≥75 x10⁹/L then give 75%.
Delay until platelets ≥75 x10⁹/L then give 50%.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and normal AST/ALT
Vincristine: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurotoxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine dose to 1mg. For higher grades or if toxicity worsens despite reduction, stop vincristine.

pre_medications: Prednisolone 50mg oral morning of treatment (patient self-administers; check prior to chemotherapy)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (days 1 and 15)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (days 8 and 22)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for bleomycin-related reactions
special_warnings: Complex 28-day 8-drug alternating weekly regimen: Days 1+15 receive R, Cyclophosphamide, Doxorubicin, Etoposide, Prednisolone; Days 8+22 receive Bleomycin, Methotrexate, Vincristine.
Bleomycin maximum cumulative dose: 500,000 IU in patients <60 years.
Doxorubicin maximum lifetime cumulative dose: 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
Vincristine maximum single dose: 2mg.
GCSF must be OMITTED on days of bleomycin administration to reduce pulmonary toxicity risk.
Folinic acid rescue 15mg oral 6-hourly x6 doses starting 24 hours after methotrexate on days 8 and 22.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Baseline LVEF required for patients with cardiac history. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Pulmonary function tests recommended before starting; repeat if respiratory symptoms develop.
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration.
Haematological dose modifications apply to days 1 and 15 only; days 8 and 22 given irrespective of counts.
Anti-infective prophylaxis: aciclovir 400mg BD, co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri.
Allopurinol 300mg OD first cycle for TLS prophylaxis.
FBC, LFTs, U&Es prior to day 1 and day 15; albumin prior to each cycle.

disease_site: CD20-positive Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma (patients unsuitable for anthracyclines)

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).
IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.
In 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Irritant.

IV bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Vesicant. Replaces doxorubicin for patients unsuitable for anthracyclines. Cardiac toxicity more likely at cumulative doses >160mg/m² (>100mg/m² after prior anthracycline therapy). Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

50mg once daily oral days 1-28 inclusive in cycle 1. From cycle 2 onwards: 50mg on alternate days. To be taken in the morning with or after food. Patient self-administers; check prior to rituximab administration.

10,000 International Units/m² IV bolus over 10 minutes. Dose rounded to nearest 1000 IU (up if halfway). Maximum cumulative dose 500,000 IU in patients <60 years. GCSF must be omitted on days of bleomycin administration.
IV bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Maximum dose 2mg. Rounded to nearest 0.1mg (up if halfway). Vesicant. NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose. Day 8 and 22 given irrespective of haematological counts.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-0.9 x10⁹/L (day 1/15) - 1st occurrence, curative intent, WHO PS ≤2, no prior GCSF
dose_modifications.notes: Administer 100% with prophylactic growth factors. Alternative: delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then 75% with GCSF.
Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then reduce by further 25% with prophylactic GCSF.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (day 1/15) - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then give 75% with GCSF.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <0.5 x10⁹/L or febrile neutropenia (day 1/15) - 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L then give 50% with GCSF.
Delay until platelets ≥75 x10⁹/L then give 75%.
Delay until platelets ≥75 x10⁹/L then give 50%.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Etoposide - Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180
Hepatic: Etoposide - Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180
Hepatic: Vincristine - Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180
Hepatic: Vincristine - Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and normal AST/ALT
Hepatic: Vincristine - Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180
Vincristine: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurotoxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine to 1mg. For higher grades or if worsens despite reduction, stop vincristine.

pre_medications: Prednisolone 50mg oral morning of treatment (patient self-administers; check prior to rituximab)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (days 1 and 15)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for bleomycin-related reactions
special_warnings: RPMitCEBO is a variant of RPACEBOM for patients UNSUITABLE FOR ANTHRACYCLINES - mitoxantrone replaces doxorubicin.
Complex 28-day alternating weekly regimen: Days 1+15 receive R, Cyclophosphamide, Etoposide, Mitoxantrone, Prednisolone; Days 8+22 receive Bleomycin, Vincristine.
Bleomycin maximum cumulative dose: 500,000 IU in patients <60 years.
Vincristine maximum single dose: 2mg.
Mitoxantrone cardiac toxicity risk at cumulative doses >160mg/m² (>100mg/m² after prior anthracycline therapy).
GCSF must be OMITTED on days of bleomycin administration to reduce pulmonary toxicity risk.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Baseline LVEF required in patients with cardiac history. Discontinue mitoxantrone if cardiac failure develops.
Pulmonary function tests recommended before starting; repeat if respiratory symptoms develop.
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration.
Haematological dose modifications apply to days 1 and 15 only; days 8 and 22 given irrespective of counts.
Anti-infective prophylaxis: aciclovir 400mg BD, co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri.
Allopurinol 300mg OD first cycle for TLS prophylaxis.
FBC, LFTs (including albumin), U&Es prior to day 1 and day 15.

disease_site: NK/T-cell lymphoma (extranodal natural killer T-cell lymphoma)

drugs.notes: HIGH DOSE. In 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 360 minutes. Urinary pH must be above 7 before starting and maintained throughout infusion until methotrexate level ≤0.1 micromol/L. Folinic acid rescue 30mg IV every 3 hours starting 24 hours after start of infusion continued until levels <0.1 micromol/L. Monitor methotrexate levels every 24 hours from 48h post-infusion. DO NOT give if fluid space (pleural effusion/ascites) present. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands.
40mg oral or IV daily on days 2, 3, 4. Also given as dexamethasone 8mg pre-medication on day 1 prior to methotrexate.
In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on days 2, 3, 4. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (20mg/ml). Irritant.

In sodium chloride 0.9% 100ml over 15 minutes on days 2, 3, 4 (pre-ifosfamide uroprotection). Additional mesna 1500mg/m² mixed with ifosfamide and mesna 900mg/m² over 12 hours also given. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (100mg/ml). | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

In sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 60 minutes on days 2, 3, 4 (mixed with mesna 1500mg/m² in the same bag). Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (80mg/ml). Urine dipstick for haematuria every 4 hours on day of and day after administration. Monitor neurological toxicity - if grade 1 may reduce; if grade 2 stop ifosfamide. Methylene blue 50mg QDS IV can prevent/treat encephalopathy.

Mixed with ifosfamide in same 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% bag over 60 minutes on days 2, 3, 4. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

In sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours on days 2, 3, 4 starting immediately after ifosfamide/mesna bag. On day 4 may be replaced with oral mesna 320mg/m² (rounded up to nearest 400mg tablet) at 0, 2 and 6 hours post-ifosfamide. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

1500 units/m² IM on day 7. Preferred route is intramuscular; volume per site must not exceed 3mL in adults (divide if higher volume needed). Can be given between days 5-10 if day 7 falls on a weekend or if given as outpatient/ambulatory. Monitor for hypersensitivity for 1 hour post-dose. Discontinue if pancreatitis, thrombosis, grade 3-4 hypersensitivity reaction, or any grade 3 non-haematological toxicity. Reduce to 50% if prior grade 1-2 hypersensitivity reaction.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: delay start of cycle until neutrophils ≥1.0 x10⁹/L and platelets ≥100 x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: No dose modifications for grade ≤3 haematological toxicity on day 1; delay until minimum criteria met.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 thrombocytopenia (platelets <25 x10⁹/L) in cycle 1
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 25% in next cycle.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets must be >25 x10⁹/L prior to Pegasparagase administration
Liver: Etoposide - Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180
Liver: Etoposide - Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180
Liver: Ifosfamide - Bilirubin >20 µmol/L or AST/ALT >2.5xULN or ALP >2.5xULN
Liver: Methotrexate - Bilirubin <50 µmol/L and AST/ALT <180
Liver: Methotrexate - Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of dose. Consider prednisolone 1mg/kg/day premedication.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV on day 1 only prior to methotrexate infusion
Metoclopramide 10mg TDS for 7 days then when required oral or IV bolus
Ondansetron 8mg BD for 7 days oral or IV bolus
Folinic acid 30mg IV every 3 hours starting 24 hours after start of methotrexate infusion (continued until MTX level <0.1 micromol/L)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to Pegasparagase (when required for reactions)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV bolus when required for Pegasparagase reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg when required for Pegasparagase reactions
H2 antagonist (famotidine 20mg or nizatidine 150mg oral) 30 minutes prior to Pegasparagase on day 7
special_warnings: INPATIENT REGIMEN - requires hospital admission.
HIGH DOSE METHOTREXATE (2000mg/m²): urinary alkalinisation mandatory - pH must be >7 BEFORE starting infusion and maintained throughout until MTX level ≤0.1 micromol/L.
DO NOT give high-dose methotrexate if patient has fluid third space (pleural effusion, ascites).
Folinic acid rescue mandatory starting 24 hours after start of MTX infusion.
Monitor methotrexate blood levels every 24 hours from 48h post-infusion.
Pegasparagase (L-asparaginase): ANAPHYLAXIS RISK - monitor for at least 1 hour post-dose.
Discontinue Pegasparagase in: pancreatitis, thrombosis, grade 3-4 hypersensitivity, any grade 3 non-haematological toxicity.
Ifosfamide: haemorrhagic cystitis risk - urine dipstick every 4 hours. Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk - methylene blue may be used.
Growth factors starting day 6 continued until neutrophils >1.0 x10⁹/L.
Anti-infective prophylaxis: aciclovir 400mg BD, fluconazole 50mg OD, pentamidine 300mg nebulised every 28 days.
AVOID proton pump inhibitors while receiving high-dose methotrexate (H2 antagonists may be used instead).
Many drugs interact with IV methotrexate at these doses - check for interactions before prescribing additional medications.
In female patients consider norethisterone 5mg TDS to delay menstruation (caution: increased thrombosis risk with asparaginase).
This regimen causes marked myelosuppression in ~70% of patients (grade ≥3 neutropenia).
Pegasparagase can be given between days 5-10 if day 7 falls on a weekend or in outpatient/ambulatory setting.
FBC, LFTs (including albumin), U&Es, EDTA CrCl prior to day 1 and each Pegasparagase dose.

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30-60 minutes on days -4 and -3 (lymphodepletion). Dose banded per national dose band (2.5mg/ml). Vesicant. Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Tisacel is confirmed.

Target dose 0.6 to 6 x10⁸ cells (1.2 x10⁶ to 6 x10⁸ cells; non-weight-based dosing range). One dose comprising 1 or more infusion bags infused within 30 minutes. Must be administered gravimetrically - NOT via volumetric pump. Via Baxter non-leukodepleting filter latex-free giving set primed with sodium chloride 0.9%. Administer immediately after thawing. Autologous use only. CAR-T administration must not occur out of core hours or over a weekend. Only available at Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit.

dose_modifications.notes: No information available; use with caution.
Confirm with consultant before proceeding if signs of possible disease relapse.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to Tisacel on day 0
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to Tisacel on day 0
Metoclopramide 10mg TDS oral or IV (anti-emetic)
Ondansetron 8mg BD oral or IV (anti-emetic)
Levetiracetam 500mg BD oral from day 0 until day +30 (seizure prophylaxis)
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (max 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS - maximum 4 doses (must be prescribed in advance)
TLS prophylaxis per consultant review: allopurinol 300mg OD or rasburicase 7.5mg IV OD
special_warnings: IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS REQUIRED FOR LIFE: Patients treated with bendamustine carry a lifelong risk of transfusion-associated graft versus host disease (TA-GVHD). Blood products must be irradiated for life. Transfusion departments must be notified immediately.
Patient must be issued with Tisacel treatment alert card at all times.

This bendamustine lymphodepletion regimen is an ALTERNATIVE to Tisacel-Fludarabine-Cyclophosphamide for patients with prior Grade 4 haemorrhagic cystitis with cyclophosphamide or chemorefractory state to cyclophosphamide-containing regimen.
Prophylactic systemic corticosteroids are NOT recommended prior to CAR-T as may interfere with cellular therapy activity.
Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) - nearly all patients experience some degree. Monitor temperature, BP, oxygen saturation 4-hourly after Tisacel on day 0 then twice daily.
ICANS (Immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome) - ICE score assessed twice daily.
Four doses of tocilizumab must be available on ward prior to Tisacel infusion.
Tisacel cells must be administered gravimetrically - NOT via volumetric pump.
Anti-infective prophylaxis: aciclovir 400mg BD, fluconazole 100mg OD, pentamidine 300mg nebulised every 28 days during lymphodepletion (continued until count recovery for co-trimoxazole use).
Posaconazole 300mg OD if prolonged neutropenia or prior invasive fungal infection.
Stevens-Johnson syndrome/toxic epidermal necrolysis risk if bendamustine given with allopurinol - monitor closely.
Bendamustine metabolised via CYP1A2 - check for drug interactions.
Echocardiogram and baseline lung function required prior to initiating lymphodepletion.
Screening for HBV, HCV and HIV required before cell collection for Tisagenlecleucel manufacture.
Minimum period between last dose of conditioning chemotherapy and CAR-T infusion must elapse per patient's CAR-T schedule.
Only available at the Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit (WBMT).
This is a one-time protocol: bendamustine given days -4 and -3; Tisacel infused on day 0.
